signals.title: NZD/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry Divergence
signals.text: NZD/JPY spot at 112.591 is the focal point of a massive G10 divergence theme as NZD outperformance collides with JPY weakness. The RBNZ is in a hiking cycle at 2.5% vs BoJ at 1.0%, and the OIS market prices a +152bp carry premium favoring NZD (OCR-overnight 2.50% vs TONA 0.98%). This carry advantage fuels the bilateral divergence despite JPY's positive economic surprise index (CESI net +13). The massive weekly performance spread confirms a strong G10 theme, leaving JPY vulnerable to further carry-seeking outflows unless global risk sentiment deteriorates sharply.
signals.watch_level: Watch the next BoC policy decision on 15 Jul for broader G10 rate sentiment, followed by the ECB meeting on 23 Jul.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Positioning + Volatility + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY trades at 162.349, holding well above the critical 160.00 psychological threshold where BoJ verbal and physical intervention risks escalate. Speculative positioning is heavily short JPY with COT LF net short at -137,828 contracts, representing a highly crowded carry trade. The options market is actively hedging downside protection, with 25d Risk Reversals showing a moderate put skew of -1.28. This options skew aligns with an elevated Volatility Risk Premium (VRP) of +3.9% (IV 7.2% vs HV30 3.3%), indicating that institutional desks are paying a premium for JPY call options to hedge against a sudden, sharp unwind of the +264bp SOFR-TONA carry advantage.
signals.watch_level: A sustained break below 160.00 or a sudden spike in VIX above 25.0 triggers a rapid unwind of JPY shorts.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Positioning Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD trades at 1.4165, showing a severe cross-asset correlation break against WTI crude oil. The rolling correlation has collapsed to -0.888 vs its 252-day norm of +0.007 (a -1.99σ deviation), meaning the pair is rising on oil price weakness rather than trading on its historical neutral relationship. This move is supported by a massive +134bp OIS carry advantage (SOFR 3.62% vs CORRA 2.28%) and a negative Canadian Economic Surprise Index (CESI net -8). Speculators are heavily short CAD with COT LF net short at -92,861 contracts (20.6% long), while retail traders are heavily fighting this institutional trend with an 83% short bias on USD/CAD. This extreme retail-institutional divergence increases the risk of a short squeeze toward multi-month highs.
signals.watch_level: Watch the Bank of Canada policy decision on 15 Jul to confirm or invalidate the CAD weakness bias.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Risk Premium Extreme
signals.text: AUD/USD trades at 0.6941, exhibiting an extreme Volatility Risk Premium (VRP) of +23.0% as 1M implied volatility sits at 30.0% against a realized 30-day historical volatility of just 7.0%. This massive premium indicates that the options market is pricing in an extraordinary level of forward-looking risk relative to actual spot movement. The 25d Risk Reversals show a moderate put skew of -0.80, confirming that this hedging demand is heavily biased toward protecting against AUD downside. This defensive options pricing contrasts with a supportive +73bp OIS carry premium (AONIA 4.35% vs SOFR 3.62%) and a net long speculative position (COT LF net +21,597), suggesting institutional players are paying up for tail-risk protection while remaining long in the spot market.
signals.watch_level: Watch the next RBA policy meeting on 11 Aug to see if the central bank maintains its restrictive stance.
